subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's entire domain is the origin, propagation, and quantitative characteristics of sound waves — directly the 'describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude)' clause of C-2.7. All major sections (production via vibration, compression-rarefaction wave mechanics, graphical representation, wave equation v = λν, pitch/loudness, reflection leading to echo and reverberation, and ultrasound/infrasound applications including echolocation and sonar) are facets of this single competency. No second C-code within CG-2 is substantively addressed: C-2.5 (energy conservation, work) is referenced only at the level of 'sound carries energy' without mathematical treatment of potential/kinetic energy, and no other CG-2 domain strand is developed as an independent section.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to describe the origin, propagation, and quantitative characteristics of sound waves by modelling compression-rarefaction mechanics, applying the wave equation (v = λν), and interpreting graphical representations to explain phenomena such as echo, reverberation, and the applications of ultrasound and infrasound.
